title: Team R’tistler
text:
This is our project webpage.
Please stay tuned to follow our project activities.

Team Members

Eda CAN
Başak ÖZER
Cihangir Alperen TOKGÖZ
Ece Özlem ÖZCAN
Sena SİVRİKAYA



Project Topic
This project examines the development of the tourism sector in the Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus (TRNC) between 2015 and 2024. Our analysis focuses on tourist flows, accommodation statistics, and the relationship between these indicators and macroeconomic variables such as inflation and exchange rates. Specifically, the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the sector and the subsequent recovery process are analyzed through a data-driven approach.

title: Data
text:
Our project focuses on the tourism sector of the Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus (TRNC)[cite: 6]. [cite_start]We analyze a multi-source dataset that covers the period between 2015 and 2024, including indicators such as total tourist arrivals, hotel occupancy rates, and average stay duration.


We chose this topic because tourism is a vital sector for the TRNC economy, yet it is highly sensitive to external shocks like the COVID-19 pandemic and currency fluctuations. Our objectives are to examine the recovery process post-pandemic and explore how inflation and exchange rates are associated with tourism activity. So far, we have successfully integrated tourism indicators with economic variables to allow for a multi-perspective analysis.



The dataset was compiled from several official institutions[cite: 18]: - [cite_start]TRNC Tourism Planning Department: Statistical Yearbook 2024[cite: 19]. - [cite_start]TRNC Statistical Institute: Consumer price indices[cite: 19]. - [cite_start]TCMB EVDS: Exchange rate movements[cite: 19]. - [cite_start]World Bank WDI: Global macroeconomic indicators[cite: 19].



To prepare the data for analysis, we followed a reproducible workflow in R:
1. Importing: We loaded raw CSV files (e.g., trnc_panel_monthly.csv) containing tourism and economic metrics.
2. Cleaning & Tidying: We standardized column names, ensured date formats were consistent, and handled missing values (NAs) to maintain dataset integrity.
3. Reshaping: We merged separate datasets using common keys (Year/Month) to create a unified panel dataset.



The final processed dataset is stored in .RData format for easy loading in R sessions.
Download Final Project Data (.RData)

title: Data
text:
Our Dataset
Our project utilizes a comprehensive dataset combining tourism indicators and macroeconomic variables for Northern Cyprus (TRNC) covering the period 2015–2024.

Data Sources
To ensure a robust analysis, we merged data from multiple official sources: - Tourism Statistics: Total arrivals, bednights, occupancy rates, and average length of stay were obtained from the TRNC Tourism Planning Department Statistical Yearbooks. - Macroeconomic Indicators: - Consumer Price Index (CPI) for TRNC was sourced from the TRNC Statistical Institute. - Turkey’s annual inflation rates (CPI YoY%) were retrieved from the World Bank WDI. - Exchange rates (EUR/TRY and USD/TRY) were collected from TCMB EVDS and World Bank records.


Why This Dataset?
Tourism is a cornerstone of the TRNC economy, yet it is highly vulnerable to external shocks. We chose this dataset to investigate: 1. The quantitative impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. 2. How the fluctuations in the Turkish Lira affect the attractiveness of Northern Cyprus as a destination. 3. The relationship between inflationary pressures in Turkey and visitor behavior in TRNC.


Data Preparation (Preprocessing)
The following R code (collapsible) shows how we cleaned the raw data, handled missing values for certain CPI sub-indices, and merged different tables into a single unified panel dataset for analysis.


Loading necessary library
library(tidyverse)
The code below is a simplified version of our preprocessing pipeline
(based on our 01_build_dataset.R script):
1. Loading raw data from CSVs
2. Calculating air/sea shares
3. Merging with Macroeconomic indicators
4. Saving as .RData for reproducibility
In your local environment, run ‘R/01_build_dataset.R’
to generate the ‘trnc_panel.csv’ used in the next pages.

Our project focuses on the tourism sector of the Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus (TRNC)[cite: 6]. [cite_start]We analyze a multi-source dataset that covers the period between 2015 and 2024, including indicators such as total tourist arrivals, hotel occupancy rates, and average stay duration.


We chose this topic because tourism is a vital sector for the TRNC economy, yet it is highly sensitive to external shocks like the COVID-19 pandemic and currency fluctuations. Our objectives are to examine the recovery process post-pandemic and explore how inflation and exchange rates are associated with tourism activity. So far, we have successfully integrated tourism indicators with economic variables to allow for a multi-perspective analysis.

To prepare the data for analysis, we followed a reproducible workflow in R:
1. Importing: We loaded raw CSV files (e.g., trnc_panel_monthly.csv) containing tourism and economic metrics.
